title: Business Model Blindspots
use_when: Audit hidden assumptions and cognitive barriers in a business model. Use when a founder, executive, strategist, or product team may be trapped by product-centric thinking, marketing myopia, dominant logic, outdated assumptions, unclear customer need, a failing or unexpectedly successful business model, or asks "what are we missing", "why is this model not working", "what business are we really in", or "challenge our assumptions".
do_not_use_when: Do not use Business Model Blindspots when the request is mainly delivery planning, analytics readout, or stakeholder communication; route to the narrower Productize skill that owns that artifact instead. Do not use it when the user lacks enough context to distinguish facts, assumptions, and decision risk.
output_artifact: Business Model Blindspots business-model memo with value capture logic, risks, and next tests
framework_fit: Business Model Blindspots fits Business Model work in the Discover lifecycle when the user needs Business Model Blindspots business-model memo with value capture logic, risks, and next tests and has enough product context to make tradeoffs explicit. Use it to produce a decision-ready artifact, not a framework tour.
failure_modes: Produces Business Model Blindspots business-model memo with value capture logic, risks, and next tests without tying recommendations to the user's Discover stage, evidence, and decision pressure.
Treats Business Model Blindspots as generic Business Model advice instead of naming assumptions, missing inputs, risks, and the next validation step.
Fails to route away from Business Model Blindspots when another Productize skill owns the requested artifact more directly.

examples.prompt: Use $business-model-blindspots to turn this business context into a decision-ready Business Model Blindspots business-model memo with value capture logic, risks, and next tests.
examples.expected_artifact: Business Model Blindspots business-model memo with value capture logic, risks, and next tests
